entry.title: Washington Post
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Putin to visit Tehran; U.S. says Iran plans to send Russia drones
  • Key Islamic State figure killed in Syria drone strike, U.S. says
  • An emotional Macron defends his Uber dealings, as inquiry looms
  • U.K.’s elite Cambridge University opens inquiry into student deaths
  • How the next U.K. prime minister will be chosen
  • Photos: Japan bids farewell to Shinzo Abe
  • Who could replace Boris Johnson as British prime minister?
  • Japan bids farewell to Shinzo Abe at slain former leader’s funeral
  • Mexico’s president meets Biden amid tension over migration, fentanyl
  • Brother of disgraced Sri Lankan president blocked from fleeing country
  • U.S. to open new embassies, boost aid in Pacific as China’s sway grows
  • Recording reveals life in captivity for American held by Russian group
  • Fake cricket league hired farmers as pros, duped Russian gamblers, police say
  • Within the war between Russia and Ukraine, a war between Chechens
  • Uber Files put Macron’s neoliberalism in the spotlight
  • Iran to send hundreds of drones to Russia for use in Ukraine, U.S. says
  • White House says Iran may provide Russia with hundreds of drones
  • Russian strikes kill at least three in Kharkiv and injure dozens
  • Macron’s Uber dealings may prompt parliamentary inquiry in France
  • World population to reach 8 billion by November even as growth slows
  • Angolans mourn ex-leader; family seeks to keep body in Spain
  • Pope: I wouldn't live in Vatican or Argentina if I retire
  • UK: Monkeypox cases rise to 1,735 with most cases in London
  • UN limits aid to Syrian rebels to 6 months in a Russian win
  • Greece: Cars hit wild boars, 2 people killed in road pileup
  • This wild ‘I voted’ sticker could boost turnout, N.Y. county says
  • Sea lions send beach crowds fleeing ‘like Godzilla is chasing them’
  • A death row inmate wants to donate a kidney. Texas won’t let him.
  • A new pilot was forced to make a risky landing. Video captured his feat.
  • It’s been a hard time for librarians. Enter an epic sleepover of stuffed toys.
  • Yosemite wildfire doubles in size, threatens over 500 giant sequoias
  • Jan. 6 hearing live updates: Panel to explore how Trump summoned extremist groups to Washington
  • Post Politics Now: ‘It matters, but it’s not enough,’ Biden says of new gun law
  • Jan. 6 hearing expected to focus on link between militants, White House
  • Parkland dad disrupts White House event on gun control
  • Judge rejects Bannon’s bid to delay trial, executive-privilege claim
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • Elon Musk says Trump should ‘hang up his hat and sail into the sunset’
  • Stocks, oil prices fall, dollar spikes as investors weigh recession risk
  • At Invitation Homes, unpermitted work leaves leaky plumbing, faulty repairs, renters say
  • Uber leveraged violent attacks against its drivers to pressure politicians
  • Is Amazon Prime worth it for you?
  • Elevator condo buildings at transit-oriented community in Ashburn, Va.
  • You can build your own wood fence and get fancy with it
  • First residents move into former Fannie Mae headquarters
  • We Must Learn to Love Genetically Modified Crops
  • How Much Covid Relief Was Stolen? No One Really Knows
  • Do Cities Control Their Economic Destiny?
  • Drought for Rainmakers Threatens Job Cuts for Bankers
  • Why the Euro Has Tumbled Near Parity to the US Dollar
  • How UK’s Tories Will Elect Leader to Replace Johnson
  • How Europe Became So Dependent on Putin for Its Gas
  • Why US Sees Saudi Arabia Again as Partner, Not Pariah
  • If pols can’t make inflation better, they should at least stop making it worse
  • Republicans won’t say what they’re for. But it’s not hard to figure out.
  • The Biden administration’s bold embrace of harm reduction will save lives
  • Vanita Gupta on the future of policing and American democracy
  • Shorten the war. Send 60 HIMARS to Ukraine.
  • You might not have guessed it, but Biden’s not the one with the approval problem
  • Conservatives hope to turn back the cultural clock. Can they succeed?
  • Biden’s scheme to cap oil prices reflects his incoherent Russia policy
  • Canada’s internet outage should encourage us to dismantle our telecom oligopoly
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Jamie Raskin Brings Expertise on Right-Wing Extremism to Jan. 6 Inquiry
  • Trump Loses Support of Half of GOP Voters, Poll Finds
  • An Anti-Trump Republican Group Is Back for the Midterms
  • Relief Eludes Many Renters as Fed Raises Interest Rates
  • Is the World Really Falling Apart, or Does It Just Feel That Way?
  • Ukraine’s Demands for More Weapons Clash With U.S. Concerns
  • New Yorkers Drop Their Covid Vigilance as a BA.5 Wave Hits
  • How Elon Musk Left Twitter Worse Off Than He Found It
  • What Is Delaware’s Court of Chancery and Its Role in Elon Musk’s Twitter Deal?
  • The Business Lunch May Be Going Out of Business
  • The Ukraine War Is About to Enter a Dangerous New Phase
  • New Climate Promises, Same Old Global Warming
  • Why Overturning Roe Will Unleash a Legal Storm for the Supreme Court
  • Joe Biden Is Too Old to Be President Again
  • Crypto Is Crashing. Where Were the Regulators?
  • Democrats Should Declare a Day of Abortion Rights
  • A Mystery: More Jobs Filled, but Lower Output
  • Even if Republicans Outlaw Abortion, Americans Will Soon Rebel
  • Considering the Legacy of Shinzo Abe, On the Day of His Funeral
  • Can Elon Musk Get Out of Buying Twitter?
  • Police Search for Man After 3 Are Stabbed in N.Y.C.
  • Advanced E.V. Batteries Move From Labs to Mass Production
  • New Saudi Rules on Pilgrimages Upend a Rite for New York Muslims
  • 40 Years Later, Nevada Man Is Charged With Murdering a 5-Year-Old Girl
  • Experts Warn Against ‘Herbal Abortion’ Remedies on TikTok
  • Live Updates: Russia and Iran Grow Closer Amid Ukraine War
  • In West Africa and Beyond, Mali’s Famed Manuscripts Are Put to Use
  • Ailing Hong Kong Activist Defiant as Court Sentences Him to Prison
  • Drought Tightens Grip on Northern Italy
  • Dangerous Heat Wave Strikes China
  • Russia Repeatedly Strikes Ukraine’s Civilians. There’s Always an Excuse.
  • Ukrainian Medic’s Months in Russian Cell: Cold, Dirty and Used as a Prop
  • ‘There’s Nothing’: Scarcity Deepens Desperation in Sri Lanka
  • Security Forces in China Attack Protesters Seeking Frozen Funds
  • Rivals Vying to Replace Johnson Are Diverse in Background, Not in Plans
  • Suspect in Abe Shooting Held a ‘Grudge.’ Scrutiny Falls on a Church.
  • U.N. Yields to Russia and Extends Aid Mission to Syria for 6 Months
  • After a Pandemic Hiatus, the Bulls Are Running Again in Pamplona
  • London’s Heathrow Airport Says It Will Limit Passengers
  • Your Tuesday Briefing
  • Russia Steps Up Attacks on Civilian Areas, Even With Advance Paused
  • Russia Is Getting Iran to Send Hundreds of Drones, White House Says
  • Your Tuesday Briefing: Bank Protests in China
  • Cambridge Opens an Inquiry After Student Deaths
  • Webb Space Telescope: Biden and NASA Reveal First Image
  • Putin Extends Fast-Track Russian Citizenship Process to All Ukrainians
  • E.U. Health Agency Recommends Second Covid Booster for Older People
  • Inside a Uvalde Classroom: A Taunting Gunman and 78 Minutes of Terror
  • For Uvalde Families, a Call for Answers Grows Louder
  • Visiting the Marin County Civic Center, Designed by Frank Lloyd Wright
  • Timbers From 17th-Century Shipwreck Recovered Off Oregon Coast
  • White House to Warn Covid Not Over as BA.5 Subvariant Spreads
  • 4,000 Beagles Are Being Rescued From a Virginia Facility. Now They Need New Homes.
  • Biden Administration May Offer Second Coronavirus Boosters to All Adults
  • Crime Wave at 7-Eleven Stores in California Leaves 2 Dead, Police Say
  • Justice Department Taps Oregon Official to Run Troubled Bureau of Prisons
  • Gerald W. McEntee, Union Leader With Political Clout, Dies at 87
  • Biden Calls for More Gun Control at White House Event
  • Senate Absences Snarl Democrats’ Plans for a Quick Return to Business
  • Albuquerque Police Investigate Teenager’s Death in Standoff
  • Hutchinson Testimony Jolts Justice Dept. to Discuss Trump’s Conduct More Openly
  • Democrats Schedule Votes on Abortion Access, but Lack a Legislative Path
  • Robots Aren’t Done Reshaping Warehouses
  • China’s Economy Stumbles in the Fog of Covid War
  • In a Time of Conflict, Ukraine Entrepreneurs Make War Their Business
  • What Happens to Twitter if Elon Musk Walks Away?
  • Peloton, Seeking to Cut Costs, Will No Longer Make Its Own Bikes
  • Euro Falls Near Parity With the Dollar
  • Bill Ackman’s SPAC to Return $4 Billion to Investors
  • A Few Words From a Master Journalist
  • The Google One-Star Review Scam Targeting Restaurants
  • A Global Deal to Tax Large Corporations Is Delayed a Year
  • Francis X. Clines, Lyrical Writer for The Times, Dies at 84
  • Twitter’s Stock Falls Further as Doubts Swirl Over Musk’s Takeover
  • Oil Companies Oppose Windfall Tax in Britain Despie Record Profits
  • Does Public Art Have an Afterlife?
  • Germany on Edge as Nord Stream 1 Pipeline Goes Offline for Repair
  • What’s Next in the Elon Musk-Twitter Saga? A Court Battle
  • What Are Spam Bots and Why They’re an Issue in Elon Musk’s Twitter Deal
News from the New York Times

entry.title: Financial Times
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Wall Street recession fears stoked by patchy US economic data
  • Euro approaches dollar parity as traders brace for recession
  • The case for T-bills and patience
  • America’s new agricultural frontier: vertical farms
  • Beware the coming wave of competent populists
  • France’s great Dijon mustard crisis
  • Live: Priti Patel rules out Tory leadership bid
  • BYD shares tumble on speculation over Warren Buffett exit
  • Heathrow introduces passenger cap for first time to avert disruption
  • Peloton to halt in-house production of exercise bikes in latest U-turn
  • Wall Street billionaire’s son Alex Lasry pledges to spend big in Wisconsin Senate race
  • Tory leadership focus turns to who can take on Sunak
  • Executives seek briefings on Taiwan war risk
  • Activist investors wage record number of campaigns in Europe
  • Focus to fall on Trump and far-right groups at January 6 hearing
  • Bill Ackman winds down $4bn Spac after failing to find a target
  • PepsiCo boosts earnings outlook for second straight quarter
  • Iran plans to provide drones to Russia for Ukraine war, says US
  • Johnson departure will not affect arms to Ukraine, says UK Nato ambassador
  • First image from James Webb telescope shows most distant galaxies
  • Petronas subsidiaries seized as lawyers press $15bn claim on Malaysia
  • Lipsticks, lattes . . . and now labradors: JAB’s bet on pets
  • Episode 7: How OnlyFans changed porn
  • Pacific islands struggle to find unity in face of China’s diplomatic offensive
  • Rishi Sunak launches Tory leadership campaign with backing of top ministers
  • Money Clinic Podcast: Fraud and the fight to get your money back
  • FT reader poll: have your say on who should be the new Tory leader and UK prime minister
  • China restaurants/Haidilao: hotpot chain spin-off faces inflation test
  • Gautam Adani takes on Mukesh Ambani’s Jio with 5G bid
  • FirstFT: US accuses Iran of supplying Russia with drones
  • 24 hour parity people
  • Argentina’s new finance minister pledges to bring ‘order and balance’
  • Liz Truss pitched by backers to unite the Tory right
  • Petropavlovsk shares suspended after indebted miner files for administration
  • Serica Energy’s board rejects £1bn bid from smaller oil and gas rival Kistos
  • Europe’s economic outlook to worsen amid gas supply disruptions
  • China’s contrasting path offers the potential for uncorrelated returns
  • Why Parisians are wearing crop tops this summer
  • Business leaders warn Tory candidates over focus on corporation tax cuts
  • Macron shakes up French state media with funding overhaul plans
  • Valuations are turning realistic but risks remain
  • Abortion law: Roe vs Wade and the US constitution
  • The Supreme Court has weakened legal predictability in America
  • The changing face of gun owners in the US
  • Uefa aims to double value of US Champions League media rights
  • Companies cannot afford to be left behind in the quantum revolution
  • Elon Musk’s new moonshot: toppling corporate law
  • Twitter calls Musk’s bid to scuttle $44bn deal ‘invalid and wrongful’
  • Musk stirs up a legal Twitterstorm
  • Twitter: Musk tries to hit edit button on deal
  • These apps have eyes on your work emails
  • Biden’s ‘climate presidency’ is on the line
  • China-South Korea ETF cross-listing scheme stalls
  • Hedge funds cut back trading after LME nickel chaos
  • I’ve been defrauded - why won’t my bank refund me?
  • Audit watchdog to overhaul UK corporate governance code
  • Dollar surges and stocks tumble as traders weigh global growth outlook
  • Russian court allows Kazakh oil exports via key pipeline to continue
  • China’s Ganfeng Lithium buys lithium mines in Argentina
  • Trian: UK activist holds up unflattering mirror for Peltz
  • Female entrepreneurs may cope better in harsh investing climate
  • Can the Conservatives change to win again?
  • Covid-19 and the rental bust-up that never came
  • While politics fragments, Europe is falling into line on trade
Financial Times

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 12 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 12 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-12 15:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • New task forces set up by Hong Kong’s new administration get to work, pledge to find ways to speed up construction and meet housing targets
  • Nearly half of ethnic minority groups in Hong Kong jobless during peak of coronavirus pandemic, survey finds
  • Hong Kong Executive Council approves 2.5 per cent pay rise for civil servants without giving in to demands for more, citing need for prudence
  • Major study into effects of long Covid to be carried out by Chinese University of Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong protests: 2 teens acquitted of manslaughter over janitor’s death but found guilty of rioting
  • Hong Kong public transport firms Citybus and New World First Bus to join forces in bid to stem losses
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong public hospitals suspend some elective surgical operations as city logs more than 2,700 infections
  • Hong Kong man, 26, falls to death from flat after ‘leaning out of window to retrieve wireless earphone’
  • Hong Kong veteran activist Koo Sze-yiu sentenced to 9 months in jail for planning to display coffin bearing slogans outside Beijing’s liaison office
  • Proposed Covid-19 health code for Hong Kong will not be used for any political agenda, ‘only pandemic purposes’: John Lee
  • What you need to know about Hong Kong’s new health code system to identify coronavirus cases
  • Hong Kong’s nano flats: does falling demand point to the end of container-size abodes?
  • Hong Kong’s use of electronic tracking wristbands not ‘foolproof’ solution to deter Covid-19 patients from breaching quarantine, experts say
  • Hong Kong should offer free breast cancer screening as a life-saving priority
  • Beijing lambasts top US diplomat over ‘let Hong Kong be Hong Kong’ speech, accusing him of ‘grandstanding and hypocrisy’
  • Hong Kong considers tougher penalties for residents caught feeding wild pigeons
  • China to buy Xinjiang cotton, but state stockpiling won’t save mills from massive losses amid US ban
  • ‘Magnetic shields’ could help bacteria survive on Mars, Chinese scientists say
  • Russian telecoms giant MegaFon eyes Chinese 5G equipment after Western suppliers pull out amid war in Ukraine
  • China’s Tsinghua Unigroup completes debt restructuring, ownership change to keep afloat its major semiconductor operations
  • Jose Eduardo dos Santos: the Angolan leader who looked to China to fund reconstruction
  • Beijing protests after Taiwanese vice-president attends Shinzo Abe’s funeral
  • Taiwan to hold island-wide air raid drills simulating PLA attacks
  • China’s latest wearable tech is an air-conditioning system tailored for hazmat suits
  • China ‘not to blame’ for African debt crisis, it’s the West: study
  • Should Hong Kong give the red light to mainland-style coronavirus health codes?
  • No summer treat for China’s young gamers: Tencent says three hours a week game time limit remains
  • China’s banking-crisis victims promised money, but outcry as ‘it doesn’t solve underlying problem’
  • ‘Look with 2 eyes’: Beijing envoy urges South Korean media to report on ‘real China’
  • Coronavirus: new variants raise questions about sustainability of China’s zero-Covid policy
  • China debt: local governments target amateur investors in new round of risky credit expansion
  • Largest Chinese patrol ship in Taiwan Strait opens up diplomatic route to law enforcement: analysts
  • US and Australia must boost Indo-Pacific security cooperation as China looms: deputy prime minister
  • Sri Lanka crisis: political turmoil may have ‘big impact’ on China ties, observers say
  • Businesses confronting new US ban on Xinjiang products say it leaves them confused
  • India to surpass China as most-populous nation in 2023
  • Shinzo Abe: Abenomics not seen as the answer for China’s economic woes with reform favoured over stimulus
  • Durian fever: China’s Wang Yi raves over Malaysian Musang King cheesecake
  • Thailand close to approving chemical castration for sex offenders
  • Where is Sri Lanka’s President Gotabaya Rajapaksa? And why are protesters in his pool, in his bed, and playing his piano?
  • Marcos urged to stick to South China Sea ruling amid Philippines’ ‘sweet spot’ in US-China diplomacy
  • Shinzo Abe’s killing spotlights Unification Church’s ties with Japanese conservatives
  • Japan bids farewell to Shinzo Abe with funeral procession through heart of Tokyo
  • How Shinzo Abe’s push for reform at home and abroad made Japan stronger and safer
  • Airport staff block Sri Lanka’s president from fleeing crisis-hit country
  • Miss Universe Bhutan blazes LGBT trail as only openly gay celebrity in a land where same-sex activity was long illegal
  • Australia urges US to expand Asia military presence or face ‘catastrophic’ balance of power failure
  • Asia needs leaders of the calibre of Singapore’s Lee Kuan Yew to navigate a messy world
  • US launches Asia charm offensive to counter China’s diplomacy after Blinken-Wang Yi meet
  • South Korea’s Yonsei students under fire for suing underpaid cleaners over ‘noisy’ protests
  • WHO says Covid still global health emergency as cases up 30 per cent in fortnight
  • UK special forces killed Afghan detainees in suspicious circumstances, probe finds
  • Ukraine’s military starts counter-attack in occupied Kherson
  • China’s Ganfeng to pay nearly US$1 billion for its third lithium mine in Argentina to bolster resource for electric vehicle batteries
  • Elon Musk says it’s time for Donald Trump to ‘sail into the sunset’
  • Iran providing Russia ‘weapons-capable’ drones for war in Ukraine, US says
  • Joe Biden seeks to persuade Saudi Arabia to pump more oil as prices soar
  • Italy’s watchdog alleges TikTok breached EU privacy rules, warning the short video platform about targeted ads
  • Non-white patients get less ICU oxygen because of flawed device: study
  • First image from Nasa’s James Webb Space Telescope offers deepest-ever look at cosmos
  • Olympic star Mo Farah reveals he was taken to UK illegally using another child’s name
  • James Bond theme composer Monty Norman dies aged 94
  • New UK prime minister to replace Boris Johnson will be named on September 5
  • Vladimir Putin expands fast track Russian citizenship to all Ukraine
  • McDonald’s Russian successor hit by sanction shortages amid Ukraine war, meaning ‘no fries with that’
  • Hong Kong to benefit as China’s carbon neutral goal and urbanisation drive commodities in post-pandemic era, HKEX CEO says
  • XPeng’s robotics unit raises US$100 million with the aim of commercialising its business in two years
  • Hong Kong blockchain unicorn Animoca Brands raises another US$75 million as crypto crash persists
  • China’s Tianqi Lithium was due for drop, analysts say, as value falls US$4.5 billion ahead of Hong Kong trading debut
  • Hundreds of Hong Kong land sites with room for at least 20,000 new flats stuck in limbo as owners call for government action
  • Temasek sees more balanced risk-reward in China markets after recent setbacks of Alibaba, Didi and other tech bets
  • Covid-19 pandemic hits value of Hong Kong’s subsidised Home Ownership Scheme flats, study shows
  • R&F buys up to four years of breathing room as creditors give go-ahead for developer to group 10 offshore bonds into three amortised notes
  • Hong Kong’s listed firms set for more climate risk reporting, with roll out of new consolidated ISSB standards on the horizon
  • Why Hong Kong’s community malls are crucial to the future of the city’s retail sector
  • Chinese hotpot chain Haidilao mulls spinning off overseas restaurants, separate listing in Hong Kong
  • Alibaba, BYD drag Hong Kong stocks to six-week low as Covid-19 cases, tech fines crimp optimism
  • Twitter hits back at Musk, demands US$44 billion deal be completed as it prepares for lawsuit
  • Russian gas cuts to Europe are pushing Germany towards Asian coal, but the demand won’t last
  • Hair removal: all you need to know about sugar, wax, cream, machines and laser beams
  • 4 hot new chefs at Shanghai restaurants, from 3-Michelin-star Taian Table to YongFoo Elite and Urban Cafe
  • Sushi without wasabi? The condiment’s future is at risk because of climate change, Japanese farmers fear
  • Chilli Laugh Story movie review: Mirror’s Edan Lui leads Lunar New Year comedy that mixes family antics, naughty jokes and social commentary
  • James Bond movies were filmed there – now it’s being turned into Raffles London luxury hotel
  • Kyoto wants Japan travel to open quicker and foreign tourists to return, though not everyone yearns to see the masses again
  • Change the food court, change the world: eco-innovator Matt Reid on sustainability and his new dining concept in Hong Kong
  • When an American illegally entered China by boat – he did it ‘out of curiosity’
  • Hong Kong’s water supplies built by the British allowed the city to grow and thrive more than any other factor
  • Want to lose weight, but always hungry? Research suggests a way to help obese people feel full while eating less
  • She lied! RHOSLC’s Jen Shah set for prison after guilty plea: The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City star denied fraud claims at first, but now the telemarketing scam could cost her US$9.5 million
  • Who was Oprah Winfrey’s dad, Vernon Winfrey? The former barber, who passed away peacefully at 89, was the billionaire media mogul’s inspiration – and didn’t care if he was her biological dad or not
  • Déjà vu? Kate Middleton and George channel Diana and William at Wimbledon: the young prince’s tennis debut looked almost identical to his dad’s appearance alongside his own mum 31 years earlier
  • 10 richest God of Gamblers cast members – net worths, ranked: Hong Kong icons Andy Lau, Stephen Chow, Donnie Yen, Tony Leung and Chow Yun-fat make millions – but who’s the mystery billionaire on top?
  • Paris Haute Couture Week: Elie Saab’s gender-bending autumn/winter 2022-23 show featured men sweeping down the runway in red carpet-ready gowns and capes
  • Big fat Ambani wedding: Mukesh hosted a glam garden extravaganza for niece Isheta Salgaocar at his US$73 million Stoke Park estate, with family including Anant, Isha and Shloka Mehta in attendance
  • Who is Cameron Norrie, Britain’s handsome No 1 tennis star? The leftie who lost to Novak Djokovic was born in South Africa, has an American girlfriend and has earned over US$6.3 million in prize money
  • What do all 3 of Elon Musk’s baby mamas have in common? Meet Shivon Zilis, the Neuralink tech whizz who shares twins with the Tesla CEO, recent girlfriend Grimes and author Justine Wilson
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 12 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Liu's early header keeps Wuhan four points clear at summit
  • Islamic State leader in Syria killed in drone strike: US military
  • TikTok pauses changes to privacy policy on targeted ads in Europe
  • Thiem ends 14-month wait for Tour-level win

  • Brazilian ride-hailing app 99 to test electric car with China's BYD

  • Euro pulls back from near parity, up 0.2%
  • Crypto gaming firm Animoca Brands hits $5.5 billion valuation after $75 million fundraise
  • Euro strikes dollar parity as eurozone recession fears mount
  • Taiwan to activate stock stabilisation fund following market falls
  • Hyundai Motor, South Korea union reach tentative wage deal
  • Nomura expects Fed, ECB, BoE rate cuts next year
  • Grab refining controls so drivers can only mark arrival when at pick-up point or 'very close' to it
  • PAP does 'utmost' to field diverse team at elections, says PM Lee
  • New CNB director to be appointed on Nov 6
  • Live performances, activity booths to resume at Istana open house for National Day celebration
  • Scoot flight bound for Singapore turned back to Perth after technical fault
  • Temasek Holdings net portfolio value crosses S$400 billion for the first time
  • 1.5 million Singaporeans will receive up to S$700 in GST vouchers in August
  • Mother who scalded 5-year-old convicted of murder on appeal; father gets life term
  • S$23.5 million programme launched to better understand long-term impact of climate change on Singapore
  • Commentary: In this post-COVID economy, job seekers have more bargaining power
  • Mickelson, DeChambeau among early British Open starters
  • Manchester United Women sign former Spurs forward Williams
  • EU countries sing UEFA's praises in rebuff to Super League
  • Best for the game of golf if I win The Open, says McIlroy
  • India's javelin champion Chopra looks to break new ground at worlds
  • Argentina lose five players to injury
  • Boks revert to type for series decider against Wales

  • London's Heathrow airport caps departing passengers at 100,000 a day

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 12 Jul, 2022

entry.content:

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Guardian: 12 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
